equations.name: PageRank per page
equations.breakdown: N divs(2n) + (N-1) adds_pos(3n) + mul(d,sum)(2n) + add_pos(1-d, prod)(3n) + sub(1,d)(2n) = 5N+4
equations.note: Old cost was 4N+4 using div=1n. Updated to 5N+4 with v4 div=2n.

equations.name: TF-IDF per term-document pair
equations.note: Old cost was 5n using div=1n. Updated to 7n with v4.

equations.name: BM25 per query term
equations.breakdown: IDF(11n) + numerator(5n) + denominator(14n) + div(2n) + mul(IDF, fraction)(2n) = 34n

observations: TF-IDF at 7n is remarkably cheap for a foundational information retrieval algorithm
PageRank cost grows linearly with incoming links: 5N+4
BM25 is 34n per query term — 5× more expensive than TF-IDF
Cosine similarity grows linearly with dimension: 15d-1
